confidence_explanation: The evidence shows a clear, multi-day pattern of an authorized user (user_03) systematically downloading sensitive, confidential files from departments outside their scope (Finance and Engineering). This behavior matches the 'Insider Threat' criteria: gradual expansion of access scope, transition from reading to downloading, and access to sensitive files outside their department over multiple days. The volume of cross-department downloads (6 distinct confidential files) far exceeds normal baseline behavior and lacks any metadata indicating legitimate maintenance or ticket justification.

attack_chain.description: Initial unauthorized data exfiltration: user_03 downloads confidential Finance payroll data outside normal hours and scope.
Expansion of scope: user_03 downloads Engineering architecture documentation, indicating reconnaissance or data collection beyond Finance.
Continued exfiltration: user_03 downloads confidential Finance budget allocation data.
Continued exfiltration: user_03 downloads confidential Finance expense reports.
Continued exfiltration: user_03 downloads Engineering infrastructure diagrams.
Final exfiltration in observed window: user_03 downloads confidential Finance revenue data.

gaps: No metadata (ticket numbers, maintenance windows) found in the events to justify the cross-department access.
No logs indicating user_03's specific role permissions were temporarily elevated to allow this access.
No evidence of data transfer destination (e.g., external IP, USB mount) to confirm final exfiltration point, though 'download' action implies local retrieval.
narrative: user_03, an HR specialist, engaged in a systematic insider threat campaign over six days, downloading six confidential files from Finance and Engineering departments. This behavior deviates significantly from their baseline (avg 1 download/day, HR-only scope) and exhibits a clear pattern of premeditated data collection across multiple departments without legitimate justification.
